summary: Full E2E testing of CurlLoom (backend + frontend). All 9 backend pytest tests pass (100%). All 14 frontend routes load. All critical UI flows verified: Home hero CTAs, multi-step Quiz (6 questions + email submit), Early Access form, Contact form, Waitlist on product detail, Shop filtering, FAQ accordion, mobile menu toggle. _id is properly excluded from all admin endpoints. Resend email failures are non-blocking as designed. Zero console errors detected.
critical_code_review_comments: Admin endpoints (/api/admin/*) have NO authentication — exposes all user submissions (PII) publicly. Recommend adding API key or auth header before any public launch.
asyncio.create_task fire-and-forget tasks are not tracked; on worker shutdown they may be cancelled silently. Acceptable for best-effort emails but worth logging the task result.
ProductDetail page uses static slug routing — verify behavior for unknown slugs (currently not tested; may want a 404 fallback).
EmailStr validation works (422 on invalid email). Good. Consider rate-limiting on public POST endpoints to prevent spam writes into MongoDB.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_-prefixed records in early_access, contacts, quiz_results, waitlist collections via API calls during testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — runs with: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Quiz UI flow requires ~900ms wait between option clicks due to setTimeout(200) + AnimatePresence 300ms transition; using shorter waits causes flaky stale-step failures. All form selects (ea-hair-type, ea-concern, contact-reason) are native <select> elements. Admin endpoints are unauthenticated — keep in mind when adding security tests.
rca of the issue: No issues found. Initial quiz test failure during first run was a test-script timing issue (insufficient wait between animated transitions), not a product bug. Fixed in second test pass by extending inter-click wait to 900ms; quiz completed successfully and routine 'Perm care routine' was returned and email submitted.
